summary: McKenzie warns fellow AdWords advertisers about Google's aggressive pricing response to click-through rate dips. He discovered that even his best-performing keyword had its cost quadrupled after briefly dipping below 1% CTR due to random fluctuations. He advises proactively scrubbing keywords that fall under the 1% CTR threshold, noting that paying inflated costs to rehabilitate a keyword offers no guarantee the minimum bid will decrease.
key_points: Google quadruples keyword costs when CTR dips below 1%, even temporarily
Random statistical fluctuations in CTR can trigger permanent cost increases
Paying the inflated price to rehabilitate a keyword has no guaranteed payoff
Proactive scrubbing of underperforming keywords is essential AdWords hygiene
money_quotes: Go through your lists, look for words which are under 1% CTR, and scrub.
Google responded by quadrupling the cost of it.
Even if I pay that for weeks to rehabilitate the keyword there is no guarantee that the minimum bid will ever go down.
key_insight: Google's AdWords algorithm punishes even temporary CTR dips with steep, potentially permanent cost increases, making proactive keyword pruning essential for small advertisers.
